name: monitor-guard
description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for background / long-running shell launches (trailing `&` · `nohup`/`setsid`/`disown` · the Bash tool's `run_in_background` flag). When the rate-limit-survivable pattern is incomplete, emits non-blocking additionalContext enforcing commons @D g10 — the while-monitor pattern: (1) detach the job (`nohup`/`setsid`) so a session rate-limit death does NOT SIGHUP it, (2) write a progress log (`> /tmp/<job>.log 2>&1`) as a durable record + Monitor re-attach point, (3) attach the 🛰️ Monitor tool to the LOG (not the live pipe — token-light, re-attachable after a rate-limit), with an OS-side `while` heartbeat for liveness (no model-side sleep-poll). Stays silent when the job is already detached AND logged. Closes the rate-limit-force-kill class for general local background work; general-local sister of pod-monitor (which covers `hexa cloud` GPU pods). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_monitor_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.
